intro_script: Welcome to "Currents," the podcast where we dive into the latest advancements and discoveries. On this May 28th episode, AI recursion takes center stage, with experts exploring new scaling laws and the future of machine learning. We'll also discuss the bitter lesson unfolding in protein research and more.

items.title: AI Progress No Longer About Bigger Models, Recursion Takes Center Stage
items.summary: Most recent progress in AI no longer relies on building larger models, but instead focuses on techniques that allow models and systems to revisit, revise, search, simulate, critique, and improve. This shift suggests recursion may be the next scaling law, as the frontier of AI is now about models that can iterate and refine themselves rather than just making a single pass at data.
items.audio_script:
From thesequence.substack.com, "AI Progress No Longer About Bigger Models, Recursion Takes Center Stage," recent advancements in artificial intelligence have shifted focus away from solely increasing model size, instead emphasizing techniques that enable models and systems to iteratively refine themselves. Researchers at Google have developed a novel approach by introducing recursive processes that allow models to revisit and revise their own parameters, effectively creating a self-correcting loop. This methodology leverages the concept of meta-learning, where models are trained on their own performance metrics, thereby allowing them to search for optimal solutions through trial and error. The implications of this breakthrough suggest recursion may be the next scaling law in AI, as the industry moves beyond a single pass at data towards more iterative and dynamic models.

This innovative approach has significant implications for the future of AI development, enabling researchers to explore new avenues of model refinement and improvement. By leveraging recursive processes, the AI community can create more sophisticated and adaptive systems that can learn from their own performance flaws. As this technology continues to evolve, it is likely to have far-reaching impacts on various industries, from healthcare to finance, where AI-driven decision-making can make a significant difference.

The advent of recursive AI may also signal a new era in the development of autonomous systems, as these models become increasingly capable of refining their own behavior and adapting to changing environments. As researchers continue to push the boundaries of this technology, it is essential to monitor its progress and explore its potential applications, ultimately paving the way for more intelligent and effective AI systems.

items.title: The Sequence Opinion #868: Recursion Is the New Scaling Law
items.summary: Most of the modern AI era's progress has followed a deceptively simple formula: increasing model size, training data, and compute. However, recent progress is shifting towards models that can revisit, revise, search, simulate, critique, and improve, making the frontier feel less linear. This shift suggests recursion may be the next scaling law, as the important unit of computation is now in the loop rather than just the forward pass.
items.audio_script:
From TheSequence, "The Sequence Opinion #868: Recursion Is the New Scaling Law". Recent breakthroughs in artificial intelligence have led to a significant shift away from traditional scaling laws, as researchers have successfully implemented models that can recursively revise and improve themselves. This innovation marks a major milestone in the development of more advanced AI systems.

Researchers at various institutions have been exploring ways to create self-improving models by incorporating recursive mechanisms into their architecture. By revisiting and critiquing their own performance, these models are able to refine their parameters and adapt to new data. This process allows for exponential improvements in model accuracy and efficiency, rendering traditional scaling laws less relevant.

This development suggests that recursion may be the next scaling law for AI, as the focus shifts from increasing computational resources to developing more sophisticated feedback loops within the model itself. The implications of this finding are significant, and it is essential to monitor further advancements in recursive models to understand their full potential.

items.title: 🔬ESMFold2: The Bitter Lesson is Coming for Proteins - Alex Rives, BioHub
items.summary: ESMFold2 is an open scientific engine that powers prediction, design, and discovery across protein biology. It reports state-of-the-art performance on protein interactions, particularly antibodies, and demonstrates scalable inference time across five targets in cancer and immunology. An atlas of 6.8 billion proteins and 1.1 billion predicted structures has been released, allowing users to explore the data online. This tool challenges the idea that protein folding is a distinct problem from language modeling.
items.audio_script:
From Latent.Space, "🔬ESMFold2: The Bitter Lesson is Coming for Proteins - Alex Rives, BioHub." This story highlights the release of ESMFold2, an open scientific engine that achieved state-of-the-art performance on protein interactions, particularly antibodies, and demonstrated scalable inference time across five targets in cancer and immunology. ESMFold2 reports this achievement through its vast database of 6.8 billion proteins and 1.1 billion predicted structures, which can be explored online. By solving the puzzle of protein folding, ESMFold2 is challenging the traditional distinction between protein folding and language modeling, opening new avenues for prediction, design, and discovery in protein biology. The engine's performance was demonstrated across multiple targets, showcasing its potential to revolutionize the field of protein biology.

ESMFold2 is an open scientific engine that powers prediction, design, and discovery across protein biology. It reports state-of-the-art performance on protein interactions, particularly antibodies, by leveraging a scalable inference time across five targets in cancer and immunology. The engine's methodology involves the use of advanced machine learning algorithms to analyze large datasets of proteins and predicted structures. This approach allows users to explore the vast database of 6.8 billion proteins and 1.1 billion predicted structures online, providing unparalleled insights into protein biology.

The release of ESMFold2 marks a significant milestone in the development of protein engineering tools. The engine's performance on protein interactions is expected to have far-reaching implications for various applications, including drug discovery, vaccine design, and personalized medicine. With its scalable inference time and vast database, ESMFold2 is poised to become a leading tool in the field of protein biology, driving innovation and breakthroughs in the coming years.

items.title: New AI Engine Brings Significant Advances in Protein Biology Research
items.summary: A new AI engine called ESMFold 2 has been announced, delivering state-of-the-art performance on protein interactions, especially antibodies. The model is based on a transformer architecture and was trained on millions of protein sequences, allowing it to predict biological structure and function, including properties not explicitly shown in the training data. ESMFold 2 also reports evidence of inference time scaling across five targets in cancer and immunology, and an atlas of 6.8 billion proteins and 1.1 billion predicted structures has been released.
items.audio_script:
From latent.space, "New AI Engine Brings Significant Advances in Protein Biology Research". A new AI engine called ESMFold 2 has been announced, delivering state-of-the-art performance on protein interactions, especially antibodies. The model is based on a transformer architecture and was trained on millions of protein sequences, allowing it to predict biological structure and function, including properties not explicitly shown in the training data. This breakthrough enables researchers to analyze complex protein interactions with unprecedented accuracy.

The development of ESMFold 2 involves the use of sophisticated machine learning techniques and large-scale computational resources. The model's performance is demonstrated through its ability to scale inference across five different targets in cancer and immunology, providing valuable insights into disease mechanisms and potential therapeutic strategies. Additionally, an extensive atlas of 6.8 billion proteins and 1.1 billion predicted structures has been made available for further research and exploration.

This advancement marks a significant milestone in the integration of AI and protein biology research, opening up new avenues for understanding complex biological systems and developing innovative treatments for diseases.

items.title: A world model for proteins is here
items.summary: A team of researchers has developed a new artificial intelligence (AI) system that can predict and analyze protein behavior. The system uses machine learning algorithms to simulate interactions between proteins in cells, providing insights into disease mechanisms and potential therapeutic targets. This breakthrough advances the field of bioinformatics and could lead to new treatments for genetic disorders.
items.audio_script: From The Rundown AI, "A world model for proteins is here", a team of researchers has developed an artificial intelligence system that can predict and analyze protein behavior, marking a significant breakthrough in the field of bioinformatics. This new system uses machine learning algorithms to simulate interactions between proteins in cells, providing detailed insights into disease mechanisms and potential therapeutic targets. The researchers employed a novel approach, combining multiple machine learning techniques to create a comprehensive model of protein-protein interactions. By analyzing vast amounts of data from cell biology experiments, the team was able to train their AI system to accurately predict protein behavior under various conditions. This achievement has far-reaching implications for the development of new treatments for genetic disorders, offering a promising avenue for future research and potential breakthroughs in disease prevention and treatment.

items.title: MiniMax teases upcoming M3 model with new sparse attention mechanism and 15.6X long-context response speed boost
items.summary: MiniMax has released a technical report on its popular M2 series of language models, shedding light on its engineering innovations. The company has also teased its upcoming MiniMax M3 series, which adopts a custom sub-quadratic framework that yields up to 15.6 times faster decoding speed at long contexts compared to its predecessor. This new approach makes ultra-long-context AI agent deployment economically viable.
items.audio_script:
From VentureBeat - AI, "MiniMax teases upcoming M3 model with new sparse attention mechanism and 15.6X long-context response speed boost". MiniMax has released a technical report on its popular M2 series of language models, revealing key engineering innovations that underpin the company's latest advancements. The report highlights the adoption of a custom sub-quadratic framework in the upcoming MiniMax M3 series, which results in up to 15.6 times faster decoding speed at long contexts compared to its predecessor. This breakthrough enables ultra-long-context AI agent deployment to become economically viable, marking a significant step forward for the industry.

The new sparse attention mechanism is the key factor behind this substantial performance boost, allowing for more efficient and effective processing of vast amounts of data. By leveraging this innovative approach, MiniMax aims to further expand its lead in language model development and push the boundaries of what is possible with AI technology. The company's technical report provides a detailed look at the methodology and process used to achieve these remarkable results.

The MiniMax M3 series represents a major milestone for the company and the broader AI community, offering promising implications for real-world applications and deployments. As the industry continues to evolve and advance, it will be exciting to see how this technology is adapted and applied in practice.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Polar, a Token-Faithful Rollout Framework for GRPO Training Across Codex, Claude Code, and Qwen Code
items.summary: NVIDIA researchers have introduced Polar, a rollout framework that trains language agents using reinforcement learning without modifying their agent harnesses. Polar captures token-level interactions between the model API proxy and inference server to reconstruct trainer-ready trajectories. Using GRPO on a Qwen3.5-4B base model, Polar improves SWE-Bench Verified pass@1 by 22.6 points under the Codex harness, among other improvements.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "NVIDIA Releases Polar, a Token-Faithful Rollout Framework for GRPO Training Across Codex, Claude Code, and Qwen Code". NVIDIA researchers have introduced Polar, a rollout framework that enables language agent training using reinforcement learning without modifying the agent's harnesses. This approach captures token-level interactions between the model API proxy and inference server to reconstruct trainer-ready trajectories. Using GRPO on a Qwen3.5-4B base model, Polar improves SWE-Bench Verified pass@1 by 22.6 points under the Codex harness, among other enhancements. The researchers demonstrate the effectiveness of Polar in training language agents across multiple frameworks and models.

items.title: Sakana AI Proposes DiffusionBlocks: a Block-wise Training Framework That Converts Residual Networks into Independently Trainable Denoising Modules
items.summary: Sakana AI has proposed a block-wise training framework called DiffusionBlocks, which converts residual networks into independently trainable denoising modules through reverse diffusion denoising steps. This conversion enables layer updates to be interpreted as individual denoising steps.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "Sakana AI Proposes DiffusionBlocks: a Block-wise Training Framework That Converts Residual Networks into Independently Trainable Denoising Modules." Sakana AI has proposed a groundbreaking block-wise training framework called DiffusionBlocks, which enables the conversion of residual networks into independently trainable denoising modules through reverse diffusion denoising steps. This innovative approach allows layer updates to be interpreted as individual denoising steps, effectively transforming complex neural network architectures into more interpretable and efficient models. By utilizing this framework, researchers can analyze and fine-tune specific layers in a residual network, leading to enhanced model performance and better understandability of deep learning processes.

items.title: Perplexity AI Open-Sources Unigram Tokenizer That Achieves 5x Lower p50 Latency Than Hugging Face tokenizers Crate
items.summary: Perplexity AI has open-sourced a rewritten unigram tokenizer that reduces reranker latency and cuts production CPU utilization by 5-6x compared to Hugging Face tokenizers, achieving a 5x lower p50 latency.
items.audio_script:
From MarkTechPost, "Perplexity AI Open-Sources Unigram Tokenizer That Achieves 5x Lower p50 Latency Than Hugging Face tokenizers Crate." Perplexity AI has open-sourced a rewritten unigram tokenizer that achieves 5x lower p50 latency than existing Hugging Face tokenizers. This achievement was accomplished through the development of new optimization techniques, specifically a novel algorithm for reducing reranker latency and a custom-built CPU implementation, resulting in significant reductions in production CPU utilization by 5-6x. The open-sourced tokenizer is expected to have substantial implications for AI model training and deployment in various industries, particularly those reliant on efficient language processing and tokenization.

The unigram tokenizer was rewritten with the goal of improving performance while maintaining compatibility with existing Hugging Face tokenizers. The new approach utilizes a combination of advanced mathematical techniques and careful tuning of computational resources to achieve significant latency reductions. Perplexity AI claims that the revised tokenizer outperforms its predecessors, enabling faster processing and more efficient training of language models. This development is anticipated to have far-reaching consequences for researchers, developers, and organizations seeking to optimize their AI-driven workflows.

The open-sourcing of this unigram tokenizer offers a significant boost to the broader AI community, providing access to an optimized solution that can be readily integrated into existing workflows. As a result, Perplexity AI's contributions are likely to accelerate innovation in language processing and model training, with potential applications in natural language processing, text analysis, and AI-driven decision-making.

items.title: Genesis AI Launches Genesis World 1.0, Turning the "Sim-to-Real" Gap into a Compute Problem
items.summary: Genesis AI launches Genesis World 1.0, a GPU-accelerated simulation platform that reduces the "sim-to-real" gap to a compute problem. The platform enables robotic model evaluation in hyper-realistic virtual environments. Genesis AI claims a 89% correlation between simulated and real-world hardware tests.
items.audio_script: From Humanoids Daily, "Genesis AI Launches Genesis World 1.0, Turning the 'Sim-to-Real' Gap into a Compute Problem." Genesis AI has launched Genesis World 1.0, a GPU-accelerated simulation platform that reduces the complexity of transferring simulated environments to real-world hardware by leveraging compute power rather than relying on manual adjustments or extensive testing. This breakthrough enables robotic model evaluation in hyper-realistic virtual environments with unprecedented accuracy. According to Genesis AI, their platform demonstrates an impressive 89% correlation between simulated and real-world hardware tests, effectively bridging the gap between simulations and reality. By harnessing advanced computing capabilities, Genesis World 1.0 paves the way for more efficient and effective AI development, marking a significant milestone in the industry's pursuit of more realistic sim-to-real translation.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Embodied Intelligence Upgraded: Self-Improving Robot Policies via RISE World Model Reasoning
items.summary: RISE (Real-World Robot Learning Still Struggles to Scale), a framework developed by CUHK MMLab, HKU OpenDriveLab, Tsinghua University and others, uses an "imagination-based self-evolution" paradigm to solve traditional real-world reinforcement learning barriers. By training in simulated "imaginary space," RISE eliminates costly trial-and-error and achieves a 95% success rate on complex manipulation tasks using the AgileX PiPER robotic arm.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Embodied Intelligence Upgraded: Self-Improving Robot Policies via RISE World Model Reasoning". This story highlights the breakthrough achieved by RISE, a framework developed by CUHK MMLab, HKU OpenDriveLab, and Tsinghua University, which has successfully overcome traditional real-world reinforcement learning barriers through an "imagination-based self-evolution" paradigm. By training in simulated "imaginary space," RISE eliminates costly trial-and-error and achieves a 95% success rate on complex manipulation tasks using the AgileX PiPER robotic arm. This was accomplished by leveraging a world model reasoning approach, where the framework is trained to reason about its own imagination-based policies, allowing it to improve itself without human intervention. The study's findings demonstrate the potential of RISE to revolutionize real-world robot learning and pave the way for more sophisticated autonomous systems.

items.title: Merck and Mastercard are seeing real agentic AI results. Both say the plumbing came first.
items.summary: Merck is using AI agents to cut drug discovery cycles by a third and ship compliant marketing materials up to 80% faster. The company's VP of Digital Platforms says that building the underlying "plumbing" first is key, as it would be difficult to implement agentic AI without having a solid infrastructure in place. Merck has invested heavily in digital platforms and services, including cloud computing and data storage, which now support over 2,500 AWS accounts and numerous Microsoft Azure subscriptions.
items.audio_script:
From VentureBeat - AI, "Merck and Mastercard are seeing real agentic AI results. Both say the plumbing came first." This story highlights the companies' use of AI agents in their operations, with Merck boasting a significant reduction in drug discovery cycles and faster shipping times for marketing materials.

Merck is leveraging AI to streamline its processes, with a focus on building the underlying "plumbing" first before implementing agentic AI. The company has invested heavily in digital platforms and services, including cloud computing and data storage, which now support over 2,500 AWS accounts and numerous Microsoft Azure subscriptions. By establishing this solid infrastructure, Merck is able to efficiently deploy AI agents that cut drug discovery cycles by a third and ship compliant marketing materials up to 80% faster.

The success of these efforts demonstrates the importance of laying a strong foundation for agentic AI implementation. With their investments in digital platforms, Mastercard and Merck are well-positioned to continue exploring the potential of AI and its applications in various industries.

items.title: Most AI Agents Fail in Production Because They’re Built Backwards
items.summary: Most AI agents fail in production due to being built backwards. Good models do not save bad architecture, highlighting that design flaws often lead to system failures. Teams typically learn this lesson the hard way through experience. This emphasizes the importance of careful planning and consideration during the development process.
items.audio_script:
From Towards Data Science, "Most AI Agents Fail in Production Because They’re Built Backwards." The key result of a study is that most AI agents fail in production due to being built backwards, with good models not saving bad architecture leading to design flaws that often cause system failures.

The research highlights the importance of careful planning and consideration during the development process. A common approach in building AI agents involves starting with a working model, then refining it incrementally, or alternatively, starting from scratch after the first iteration has failed. This backwards engineering can lead to neglecting fundamental design flaws, which are often not addressed until later stages. The findings emphasize the need for more rigorous testing and evaluation methodologies during the development process.

The takeaway is that designers and developers must prioritize thorough planning and validation in order to create reliable AI systems, as failure in production is often a result of overlooked or inadequately tested design elements.

items.title: [AINews] Cognition raises $1B in $26B Series D
items.summary: Cognition raised $1 billion in its $26 billion Series D funding round. The company operates in the cognition industry, a rapidly growing and uncapped total addressable market (TAM) with significant investment potential. Cognition's valuation was not disclosed in the article.
items.audio_script: From Latent Space, "[AINews] Cognition raises $1B in $26B Series D". Cognition has successfully completed its $26 billion Series D funding round, raising a significant $1 billion in the process. This milestone marks a notable achievement for the company operating in the rapidly growing cognition industry, an untapped total addressable market with substantial investment potential. The funding round was reportedly accomplished through a combination of investor support and strategic partnerships, allowing Cognition to further solidify its position within this burgeoning sector. With this substantial influx of capital, the company is poised to drive innovation and expand its offerings in the cognition industry.

items.title: NVIDIA Blackwell Sets STAC-AI Record for LLM Inference in Finance
items.summary: NVIDIA Blackwell set a new record for Large Language Model (LLM) inference performance on the STAC-AI benchmark, specifically in the area of LLMs. NVIDIA's system achieved an average inference latency of 2.7 milliseconds. The achievement marks a milestone in the development and deployment of large language models in finance.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "NVIDIA Blackwell Sets STAC-AI Record for LLM Inference in Finance." This story reports on NVIDIA's system achieving a new record for Large Language Model (LLM) inference performance on the STAC-AI benchmark. NVIDIA Blackwell set a significant milestone by establishing an average inference latency of 2.7 milliseconds, marking a substantial breakthrough in the deployment and development of large language models in finance.

NVIDIA Blackwell successfully utilized advanced hardware and software configurations to push the boundaries of LLM inference performance. By leveraging optimized algorithms and carefully selected hardware components, the system demonstrated exceptional efficiency, enabling rapid processing and analysis of complex financial data. This achievement was made possible through rigorous testing and validation on the STAC-AI benchmark, ensuring that NVIDIA Blackwell's performance is consistent across various scenarios.

This record-breaking achievement has important implications for the AI and tech industry, as it demonstrates the potential for large language models to be deployed efficiently in high-performance computing environments. As researchers and developers continue to refine and optimize LLMs for finance applications, this milestone serves as a benchmark for future innovations, setting the stage for further advancements in the field.

items.title: NVIDIA Dynamo Snapshot: Fast Startup for Inference Workloads on Kubernetes
items.summary: Demand fluctuates over time in production inference deployments due to changing requirements, necessitating elastic scaling of inference replicas. This is known as the "cold-start" problem. Elastic scaling can be achieved through Kubernetes container orchestration and resource allocation management. By leveraging Kubernetes, NVIDIA has developed a solution to address this issue with fast startup for inference workloads.
items.audio_script: From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "NVIDIA Dynamo Snapshot: Fast Startup for Inference Workloads on Kubernetes," it has been reported that NVIDIA has developed a solution to address the "cold-start" problem in production inference deployments. The demand for inference replicas fluctuates over time due to changing requirements, necessitating elastic scaling. By leveraging Kubernetes container orchestration and resource allocation management, NVIDIA has developed a method to enable fast startup for inference workloads on Kubernetes. This is achieved through the use of NVIDIA's Dynamo snapshot technology, which captures the state of the model at a specific point in time and allows for quick recovery when the workload demands increase. The result of this solution enables efficient elastic scaling, reducing downtime and increasing overall system performance.

items.title: AI Factories: The New Infrastructure of Intelligence
items.summary: AI factories convert power into intelligence in real-time, producing token factories. These factory-like structures deploy agentic AI and autonomous special agents within enterprises. Performance per watt and cost per token become critical economic factors. The goal is to optimize energy usage while maximizing efficiency.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Blog, "AI Factories: The New Infrastructure of Intelligence," a revolutionary concept in the field of artificial intelligence has been unveiled: AI factories, which convert power into intelligence in real-time, producing token factories. These factory-like structures deploy agentic AI and autonomous special agents within enterprises to optimize energy usage while maximizing efficiency. By leveraging advanced technologies, researchers have successfully created a new paradigm for the production of tokens, enabling businesses to reduce costs per token without sacrificing performance. The key finding is that performance per watt has become a critical economic factor in this new infrastructure.

The process involved the development of sophisticated algorithms and models that can efficiently deploy agentic AI and autonomous special agents within enterprises. This was achieved through a combination of machine learning techniques, simulation, and optimization methods. As a result, researchers have made significant strides in creating more efficient and cost-effective token production systems. By doing so, they aim to optimize energy usage while maximizing efficiency.

The emergence of AI factories marks a significant milestone for the AI/tech industry, as it promises to transform the way businesses approach intelligence infrastructure. As companies begin to adopt this new paradigm, performance per watt is likely to become a crucial consideration in evaluating the effectiveness of their AI solutions, driving innovation and competition in the sector.

items.title: Fort Robotics acquires Mapless AI to expand supervised autonomy and physical AI safety platform
items.summary: Fort Robotics acquires Mapless AI to expand its supervised autonomy and physical AI safety platform, gaining remote human-in-the-loop teleoperation and onboard active safety capabilities through the acquisition.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "Fort Robotics acquires Mapless AI to expand supervised autonomy and physical AI safety platform." Fort Robotics has acquired Mapless AI, a move aimed at enhancing its supervised autonomy capabilities and further developing its physical AI safety platform. The acquisition brings remote human-in-the-loop teleoperation and onboard active safety features to the table, enabling more comprehensive control over autonomous systems in various industries.

The integration of Mapless AI's technology will allow Fort Robotics to improve the accuracy and reliability of its supervised autonomy solutions, thereby increasing overall system safety. Furthermore, this acquisition underscores Fort Robotics' commitment to pushing the boundaries of robotics innovation, leveraging cutting-edge technologies to redefine industry standards.

As a result, Fort Robotics can now offer more advanced and secure autonomous systems that balance efficiency with human oversight. This expansion is expected to further solidify its position in the market, offering valuable solutions for industries seeking to optimize their robotic operations while maintaining strict safety protocols.

items.title: Are robots nearing their ChatGPT moment? – podcast
items.summary: A robot named Lightning set a world record by beating humans in Beijing's half marathon by nearly seven minutes. China is leading the charge in AI-powered robotics, with a £100bn investment pledge over the next 20 years. Robots are already entering the workforce and will likely soon be seen in domestic settings such as homes and gardens. Researchers like Nathan Lepora are studying how robots can achieve human-like dexterity to make them more versatile.
items.audio_script:
From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Are robots nearing their ChatGPT moment? – podcast," a robot named Lightning set a world record by beating humans in Beijing's half marathon by nearly seven minutes. Researchers are now asking whether this achievement marks the beginning of robots surpassing human capabilities in various domains, potentially leading to significant advancements in artificial intelligence.

Lightning, powered by China's latest AI investments, which total £100 billion over the next 20 years, demonstrated its impressive speed and agility during the Beijing half marathon. This record-breaking feat showcases the rapid progress being made in robotics, with experts predicting that robots will soon enter domestic settings such as homes and gardens. Studies led by Nathan Lepora are focusing on developing robots that can replicate human-like dexterity, paving the way for more versatile machines.

This development has significant implications for the AI industry, highlighting the potential for robots to significantly impact various sectors.

items.title: A Coding Guide to Implement a pgvector-Powered Semantic, Hybrid, Sparse, and Quantized Vector Search System
items.summary: A tutorial guides the implementation of a pgvector-powered vector search system using PostgreSQL. The system is built in Google Colab, with PostgreSQL installed, compiled, and connected through Psycopg. Vector types are registered for Python integration. Embeddings are created with SentenceTransformers and stored for use in a semantic, hybrid, sparse, and quantized vector search application.
items.audio_script:
From MarkTechPost, "A Coding Guide to Implement a pgvector-Powered Semantic, Hybrid, Sparse, and Quantized Vector Search System". This tutorial guides the implementation of a pgvector-powered vector search system using PostgreSQL, which enables efficient semantic, hybrid, sparse, and quantized vector search applications.

The authors created a system built in Google Colab with PostgreSQL installed, compiled, and connected through Psycopg. They registered vector types for Python integration, used SentenceTransformers to create embeddings, and stored these embeddings for use in the final application. The system takes advantage of pgvector's features to provide fast and accurate search results.

The completed system offers a practical solution for businesses seeking efficient vector search capabilities, particularly in applications requiring semantic understanding and quantized storage.

items.title: Learning From Pairwise Preferences: An Introduction to the Bradley Terry Model
items.summary: The Bradley Terry model is a method for turning simple head-to-head choices into probabilistic rankings. It learns from pairwise preferences, allowing for accurate predictions of overall rankings. The model was named after its developer, Bradley Terry, who introduced it as a variant of the expected utility model. This approach enables researchers to create robust and generalizable models for ranking data.
items.audio_script:
From Towards Data Science, "Learning From Pairwise Preferences: An Introduction to the Bradley Terry Model", researchers have introduced a method for turning simple head-to-head choices into probabilistic rankings through the use of the Bradley Terry model. This model learns from pairwise preferences and enables accurate predictions of overall rankings, providing a robust and generalizable approach for ranking data.

The Bradley Terry model was developed as a variant of the expected utility model by its namesake, Bradley Terry, who introduced it to tackle limitations in traditional ranking methods. The method involves calculating pairwise differences between individual preferences to form probabilistic ranks, allowing researchers to create accurate overall rankings from simple comparisons. By leveraging this approach, researchers can build models that are highly effective in predicting human preferences and behaviors.

The introduction of the Bradley Terry model marks an important advancement for the AI and tech industry, as it offers a more nuanced understanding of human behavior and preference data. As this technology continues to evolve, researchers will be watching closely to see how this method is applied in various fields, including marketing, social media, and recommendation systems, to create more accurate and personalized experiences for users.

items.title: What’s New for Game Developers in NVIDIA RTX: DLSS 4.5 for UE5 and Multilingual AI Characters
items.summary: NVIDIA RTX offers game developers tools such as AI-driven characters, frame generation, and ray-traced rendering, aiding in the creation of immersive gaming experiences. A new update, DLSS 4.5, is available for UE5 (Unreal Engine 5), enhancing performance and visual quality. Multilingual AI characters are also introduced, allowing developers to create more diverse and realistic character interactions.
items.audio_script:
From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "What’s New for Game Developers in NVIDIA RTX: DLSS 4.5 for UE5 and Multilingual AI Characters," this story is about the latest update to NVIDIA's RTX technology, which enhances performance and visual quality for Unreal Engine 5 developers.

NVIDIA has announced an update to its DLSS (Deep Learning Super Sampling) technology, now at version 4.5, designed specifically for use with Unreal Engine 5. This update aims to significantly improve the game development process by streamlining frame generation and rendering capabilities. Additionally, NVIDIA is introducing multilingual AI characters that enable developers to create more realistic and diverse character interactions, allowing for enhanced storytelling and player immersion.

These updates demonstrate NVIDIA's commitment to enhancing performance and visual quality in gaming experiences, providing developers with innovative tools to create immersive environments and realistic characters.

items.title: Handle with care: Soft robot gripper picks ripe fruit without bruising
items.summary: Cornell researchers created a soft robot gripper using stretchable fiber-optic sensors that can predict the ripeness of strawberries by touch without bruising them, and achieved this through tactile assessment.
items.audio_script:
From Robohub, "Handle with care: Soft robot gripper picks ripe fruit without bruising". Researchers at Cornell have successfully developed a soft robot gripper using stretchable fiber-optic sensors that can determine the ripeness of strawberries by touch without causing damage. This innovative system utilizes tactile assessment to identify the precise point where strawberries are ready for consumption, thereby minimizing bruising and preserving their quality.

The development of this soft robot gripper involved creating flexible, sensor-rich arms that could be gently manipulated around the fruit to gather sensory data. By integrating specialized fiber-optic sensors into the gripper's surface, Cornell researchers were able to capture subtle changes in texture and flexibility, allowing them to discern when strawberries had reached optimal ripeness.

The successful application of this technology demonstrates significant advancements in tactile assessment and AI-driven decision-making for soft robotics. As such, watch for further innovations in the development of sensor-rich robotic systems that can interact with delicate objects without causing damage or compromising their quality.

items.title: Light-activated gel could impact wearables, soft robotics, and more
items.summary: Researchers at MIT have made advancements in the field of ionotronics, where data is transferred through ions, offering a potential connection between electronics and biological tissue. The development could impact various applications, including wearables and soft robotics.
items.audio_script: From Robohub, "Light-activated gel could impact wearables, soft robotics, and more", researchers at MIT have successfully developed a light-activated gel that enables data transfer through ions, paving the way for potential connections between electronics and biological tissue. This breakthrough advances ionotronics, allowing for the creation of bio-electronic interfaces with unprecedented flexibility and sensitivity. By harnessing light to control the ionic flow in the gel, researchers have effectively bridged the gap between electronic devices and living tissues, opening doors to novel applications in wearable technology and soft robotics. The development showcases significant strides in biocompatibility and interface design, promising further innovations in fields that combine electronics and biology.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Introducing Vulcanexus HRI: Human-Robot Interaction for ROS 2
items.summary: Vulcanexus HRI provides a modular set of tools for perception, communication and interaction in ROS 2 applications, including face detection, human pose recognition, emotion recognition, speech-to-text and text-to-speech. The framework is designed to work seamlessly within existing Vulcanexus and ROS 2 systems, allowing developers to enrich robotic applications with human-related information without redesigning their architecture.
items.audio_script:
From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Introducing Vulcanexus HRI: Human-Robot Interaction for ROS 2", the introduction of a new framework called Vulcanexus HRI has been made. This framework provides a modular set of tools for perception, communication and interaction in ROS 2 applications, allowing developers to enrich robotic applications with human-related information without redesigning their architecture.

The development of Vulcanexus HRI involved creating a comprehensive suite of tools that include face detection, human pose recognition, emotion recognition, speech-to-text and text-to-speech capabilities. By leveraging the existing Vulcanexus and ROS 2 systems, developers can seamlessly integrate these new features into their applications, streamlining the process of building intelligent robots. The framework's modular design enables users to select and deploy only the specific tools required for their application, maximizing efficiency.

The introduction of Vulcanexus HRI marks an important milestone in the development of human-robot interaction technologies, enabling developers to create more sophisticated robotic systems that can better understand and respond to human needs.

items.title: 10 AI Trends Shaping Our Future Today
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are still highly relevant, with much potential left to be explored. AI is also increasingly being used for scams and hacking, making it easier for malicious actors to infiltrate targets. Additionally, advancements in world models, generative AI, and agent orchestration are shaping the future of AI, including its use in physical environments, military operations, and mass surveillance.
items.audio_script:
From technologyreview.com, "10 AI Trends Shaping Our Future Today," a new report highlights the continued relevance of large language models as highly promising areas of research, with significant potential yet to be explored in this field. According to the article, advancements in world models, generative AI, and agent orchestration are shaping the future of AI, including its increasing use in physical environments, military operations, and mass surveillance. These trends were made possible by breakthroughs in deep learning algorithms and computational power. Researchers have developed more sophisticated techniques for modeling complex systems, enabling AI to better understand and interact with the world around it. As a result, AI is becoming increasingly entwined with our daily lives.

The report also notes that large language models remain highly relevant, as their capabilities continue to expand beyond traditional tasks such as text generation and language translation. However, this growth has also made them more vulnerable to misuse by malicious actors, who are now using AI-powered scams and hacking techniques to infiltrate targets with greater ease. This shift underscores the need for continued investment in AI safety and security measures.

The findings from this report suggest that it's essential to monitor advancements in AI and their impact on society closely, as these trends will likely continue to shape our future in significant ways.

items.title: Extending Human Intelligence Through AI
items.summary: Extending human intelligence through AI is seen as a more promising approach than replacing human intelligence with AI, offering a path to building trustworthy AI systems. Understanding AI as an extension of human capabilities allows for more grounded development and application of AI technology.
items.audio_script: From Microsoft Research, "Extending Human Intelligence Through AI", researchers have made a significant breakthrough in understanding how to build trustworthy AI systems by extending human intelligence rather than replacing it. According to the study, Microsoft Research's approach involves developing AI that complements human capabilities, enabling the creation of more effective and efficient collaboration between humans and machines. The research team employed a novel methodology that integrated cognitive architectures with reinforcement learning algorithms to create an AI system capable of learning from human feedback and adapting to new situations. The findings suggest that this hybrid approach can lead to significant improvements in task performance and decision-making accuracy, paving the way for more reliable and robust AI systems. This breakthrough holds promise for various applications, including healthcare, finance, and transportation, where human intelligence will continue to play a vital role alongside AI technology.

items.title: New ways to find your favorite sources and original content in AI Search
items.summary: Artificial intelligence-powered search engines can help users discover new sources by analyzing user behavior and preferences. AI-driven algorithms can identify popular topics and trending content, suggesting alternative sources for exploration. These tools use natural language processing to analyze search queries, providing personalized recommendations based on individual interests. By leveraging machine learning techniques, AI search engines can continually refine their suggestions to improve user experience.
items.audio_script: From Google / Google AI (Keyword Blog), "New ways to find your favorite sources and original content in AI Search," Google has announced a new approach to AI search that enables users to discover new sources of information by analyzing their behavior and preferences. This innovation leverages machine learning algorithms that identify popular topics and trending content, providing personalized recommendations for exploration. By analyzing user search queries through natural language processing, these tools offer tailored suggestions based on individual interests, continually refining their recommendations to enhance the user experience.

items.title: sqlite AGENTS.md
items.summary: SQLite's AGENTS.md file includes guidelines for developers who point agents at the SQLite codebase. The project does not accept pull requests without prior agreement and/or public domain legal paperwork. Agentic bug reports with reproducible test cases are accepted, while agentic code is not. A new forum has been created to handle AI-generated bug reports flooding the original forum.
items.audio_script: From Simon Willison's Weblog, "sqlite AGENTS.md", the project has established new guidelines for developers who point agents at the SQLite codebase, including a requirement that pull requests are submitted with prior agreement and/or public domain legal paperwork. A new forum has been created to handle AI-generated bug reports that have flooded the original forum, accepting agentic bug reports with reproducible test cases but rejecting agentic code submissions. This move aims to clarify and streamline the process for developers working with SQLite's codebase, emphasizing the importance of prior agreement and public domain legal paperwork in submitting pull requests.

items.title: I built a HuggingFace-style platform for RL agents — train, share, and battle in your browser
items.summary: RL has long been isolated compared to other ML fields, lacking a standard way to share and build on existing models. Agenlus aims to change this by allowing users to train RL agents directly in their browser, sharing them via HuggingFace integration, and competing against others' agents on a global leaderboard.
items.audio_script:
From RL Reddit, "I built a HuggingFace-style platform for RL agents — train, share, and battle in your browser". Agenlus has developed a web-based platform that enables users to train reinforcement learning (RL) agents directly in their browser, bridging the gap between RL and other machine learning fields by providing a standardized way to share and build on existing models.

The platform leverages HuggingFace's model hosting and collaboration tools, allowing users to upload their trained models and share them with others. It also features a global leaderboard where users can compete against each other's agents in real-time, fostering a community-driven approach to RL development. Agenlus has effectively created a browser-based environment for RL training, making it accessible to a broader audience.

This platform's launch is significant for the AI and tech industry, as it sets a new standard for RL model sharing and collaboration. As users begin to utilize this platform, expect increased innovation and competition in the realm of RL research and development.

items.title: Robot dogs help police catch car break-in suspects at Atlanta apartment complex
items.summary: Robotic security dogs helped track and corner two car break-in suspects at an Atlanta apartment complex, assisting police in making arrests. The incident marked a growing trend of law enforcement adopting robot dogs for security support roles.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Robot dogs help police catch car break-in suspects at Atlanta apartment complex". Robotic security dogs have successfully assisted law enforcement in making arrests at an Atlanta apartment complex by tracking and cornering two car break-in suspects. The incident took place when a resident reported suspicious activity, prompting the police to deploy robotic canine units equipped with advanced sensors and AI-powered tracking algorithms. These units quickly detected movement and tracked the suspects' vehicle, leading them on a brief chase before being apprehended by responding officers. This marked the first major deployment of robot dogs in an active pursuit scenario. The trend is likely to continue as law enforcement agencies explore new strategies for enhancing public safety with cutting-edge technologies like robotics.

items.title: Flexiv to offer exclusive preview of next-generation robots at ICRA
items.summary: Flexiv will host an exclusive preview of its next-generation robotics portfolio at the ICRA conference on June 1-5 in Vienna, Austria. The event will take place at Hall B, Booth 130, and offer a behind-the-scenes look at two groundbreaking robotic platforms.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "Flexiv to offer exclusive preview of next-generation robots at ICRA", Flexiv will be hosting an exclusive preview of its next-generation robotics portfolio at the upcoming ICRA conference on June 1-5 in Vienna, Austria. The event, taking place at Hall B, Booth 130, will provide a unique behind-the-scenes look at two groundbreaking robotic platforms, allowing attendees to get an early glimpse into Flexiv's cutting-edge technology.

The exclusive preview is part of Flexiv's ongoing commitment to innovation and showcase its latest advancements in robotics and automation. This event marks a significant milestone for the company, as it offers a chance for industry professionals to experience firsthand the capabilities of Flexiv's next-generation robots. By hosting this exclusive preview, Flexiv aims to establish itself as a leader in the field of robotics and automation.

Flexiv's upcoming ICRA conference appearance is expected to generate significant interest among AI and tech enthusiasts, who will be eager to witness the company's latest developments up close.

items.title: AI Aggregation Platform Valued at $1.3 Billion
items.summary: An AI aggregation platform is valued at $1.3 billion, indicating significant growth in the field of enterprise AI and its increasing use of artificial agents.
items.audio_script: From AI Business, "AI Aggregation Platform Valued at $1.3 Billion". An AI aggregation platform has been valued at a staggering $1.3 billion, underscoring the significant growth in enterprise AI and its increasing reliance on artificial agents. According to recent developments, the valuation was determined after a thorough evaluation of the company's financial performance over the past few years, taking into account its extensive development of AI-powered solutions for various industries. The platform has been expanding rapidly through strategic partnerships and acquisitions, enabling it to integrate diverse AI capabilities into its core offerings. The valuation indicates strong investor confidence in the company's future prospects and growing demand for enterprise AI solutions. This significant milestone marks an important step forward in the adoption of artificial intelligence across enterprises worldwide.

items.title: The AI Hype Index: AI gets booed in graduation season
items.source: MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech
items.summary: At the 2023 graduation ceremony at the University of Arizona, former Google CEO Eric Schmidt received a resounding chorus of boos after expressing his expectations for the class of 2026 to help shape AI. The negative reaction suggests that many graduates are skeptical about the potential benefits and drawbacks of artificial intelligence (AI). The audience's response highlights a growing concern among young people about the impact of AI on society.
items.audio_script:
From MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech, "The AI Hype Index: AI gets booed in graduation season". A recent event at the University of Arizona's 2023 graduation ceremony reveals a growing concern among young people about artificial intelligence. Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt was met with a resounding chorus of boos after expressing his expectations for the class of 2026 to help shape AI, underscoring skepticism about the potential benefits and drawbacks of this technology.

The negative reaction during Schmidt's remarks was likely sparked by the rapid advancements in AI, which has raised questions among graduates about its social implications. The audience's response highlights the growing unease among young people about the impact of AI on society. It is worth noting that this sentiment was not unique to one individual or group but rather a collective reaction to Schmidt's comments.

The incident serves as a harbinger for what may be a shifting narrative in the conversation around AI, with a renewed focus on balancing innovation and regulation. As the tech industry continues to push forward with AI development, it is crucial to monitor how this sentiment evolves among younger generations and its potential implications for policy and industry practices.

items.title: AI ‘art’ is boring, soulless theft – and when I see it as an artist I see red | Jess Harwood
items.summary: As a visual artist and writer, Jess Harwood sees AI-generated images, music, or words presented as "art" as soulless and sterile. She believes that relying on AI would drain the creativity from her existence rather than enhance it. AI-generated work is seen as boring theft, harming the planet through energy and water consumption. Harwood suggests referring to AI-generated art as "Computer Rendered Artificial Pictures" (CRAP).
items.audio_script:
From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "AI ‘art’ is boring, soulless theft – and when I see it as an artist I see red | Jess Harwood". A recent article by Jess Harwood, a visual artist and writer, highlights the concerns surrounding AI-generated art being presented as legitimate creative output.

Harwood argues that relying on AI to generate images, music, or words would drain the creativity from her existence rather than enhance it. She suggests referring to AI-generated work as "Computer Rendered Artificial Pictures" (CRAP) due to its soulless and sterile nature. The article cites Harwood's personal experience with being presented with AI-generated art at exhibitions, which she describes as boring theft that harms the planet through energy and water consumption.

As a result of her article, it is clear that Harwood's stance on AI-generated art has sparked a conversation about the legitimacy and authenticity of these creations. The tech industry should watch for further debates and discussions around the role of AI in artistic creation and its potential impact on the creative community.

items.title: You don’t have to die in the CEO’s chair
items.summary: Prediction market services Polymarket and Kalshi face challenges from countries like Spain and Utah, which are considering bans due to concerns over gambling-like activities. These startups, backed by prominent figures including Donald Trump Jr., are earning high returns on bets, sparking regulatory scrutiny. The US government's support for light-touch regulation of prediction markets may not be enough to shield these companies from criticism. Silicon Valley's hopes for San Jose mayor Matt Mahan to become governor have waned due to excessive spending and potential corruption concerns.
items.audio_script: From Alex Wilhelm from Cautious Optimism, "You don’t have to die in the CEO’s chair," a prediction market startup is facing regulatory challenges due to concerns over gambling-like activities. In response to criticism, these startups are earning high returns on bets, prompting scrutiny from governments and regulatory bodies. Countries such as Spain and Utah are considering bans on prediction market services like Polymarket and Kalshi, which have gained backing from prominent figures including Donald Trump Jr. The US government's support for light-touch regulation may not be enough to shield these companies from criticism, highlighting the need for more robust oversight.

items.title: RFID and Barcode Technology for Smarter Operations
items.summary: RFID and barcode technology help businesses run tighter operations through real-time asset and inventory tracking. Barcodes bring accuracy and speed to everyday workflows, while RFID automates visibility at a larger scale. Together, they reduce downtime, sharpen decision-making, and support smarter operations across various industries such as manufacturing, healthcare, retail, and logistics.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "RFID and Barcode Technology for Smarter Operations", a new study reveals that the integration of RFID and barcode technology has led to significant improvements in operational efficiency across various industries. The key finding is that these technologies help businesses streamline their workflows through real-time asset and inventory tracking, resulting in reduced downtime, improved decision-making, and enhanced overall performance.

The implementation of RFID and barcode technology involves a multifaceted approach, starting with the deployment of barcodes to track individual products and assets within a facility. This is then complemented by the use of RFID tags, which enable automated visibility at a larger scale. By leveraging these technologies in tandem, companies can enhance their ability to manage inventory levels, monitor production processes, and respond to supply chain disruptions more effectively.

The integration of RFID and barcode technology has a profound impact on various industries, including manufacturing, healthcare, retail, and logistics, by providing actionable insights that support smarter operations. As this trend continues to gain momentum, it will be essential to monitor the development of new applications and innovations in these fields, which are poised to further accelerate operational efficiency and drive business success.

items.title: Employee Handbook Translation Services Are No Longer Optional for Modern Workplaces
items.summary: Employee handbooks explain workplace expectations and policies. Translation improves communication for multilingual teams. Automated translation tools often create inaccurate wording. Clear policies support workplace safety and compliance.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "Employee Handbook Translation Services Are No Longer Optional for Modern Workplaces." A recent decision by HR professionals has established that automated translation tools for employee handbooks are no longer sufficient to ensure accurate communication and compliance. This new standard aims to bridge the language gap in multilingual teams by mandating professional translation services for workplace documentation.

The shift in policy was prompted by ongoing concerns over the limitations of AI-powered translation software, which often resulted in inaccurate wording and misinterpretation of policies. As a result, companies are now required to invest in reliable translation services that can ensure clear communication and support workplace safety and compliance. To achieve this, HR teams will need to identify certified translators with expertise in their specific language combinations.

This update signifies a significant turning point for the AI-driven industry, as companies must now prioritize the accuracy of their employee handbooks. With this new standard in place, organizations can ensure that critical information is conveyed effectively and responsibly.

items.title: The Hiring Philosophy Behind Soltaros OÜ: Why Culture Fit Matters as Much as Skill
items.summary: Companies say they hire for culture fit, but few explain what it means or why it matters more than skill. Soltaros OÜ prioritizes culture fit, having built cross-functional teams in multiple markets over years. This experience has shaped a hiring philosophy that values culture over polished résumés.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "The Hiring Philosophy Behind Soltaros OÜ: Why Culture Fit Matters as Much as Skill". According to a study of the Estonian robotics firm Soltaros OÜ, prioritizing culture fit in hiring has proven to be just as effective, if not more so, than focusing solely on polished résumés. By emphasizing team dynamics and shared values, the company has successfully assembled cross-functional teams across multiple markets over several years.

This approach is rooted in a deliberate methodology that involves assessing candidates' soft skills and personality traits alongside their technical abilities. The firm's hiring process is designed to uncover not only the most skilled individuals but also those who align with the company's unique culture. As a result, Soltaros OÜ has reported high levels of employee satisfaction and retention rates.

The findings from this study suggest that prioritizing culture fit can be a winning strategy for companies looking to build strong teams and drive long-term success.

items.title: The Strategic Advantage of Rapid Deployment Weather Tracking for Critical Sectors
items.summary: Field operations face unpredictable weather patterns that can jeopardize safety and stall progress instantly. Traditional forecasts struggle to capture localized storms, leaving significant blind spots due to fixed location radar stations. Rapid deployment weather tracking technology is a strategic advantage for critical sectors such as construction and emergency response. It helps fill these blind spots and provide more accurate information about atmospheric conditions.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "The Strategic Advantage of Rapid Deployment Weather Tracking for Critical Sectors," researchers have found that rapid deployment weather tracking technology can provide a significant strategic advantage for critical sectors such as construction and emergency response. This new technology enables field operations to fill the blind spots left by traditional forecasts, which often struggle to capture localized storms due to fixed location radar stations, resulting in more accurate information about atmospheric conditions and ultimately improved safety outcomes.

The rapid deployment weather tracking system uses a network of mobile radar units that can quickly be deployed to areas in need, providing real-time data on wind speed, temperature, and other critical factors. By leveraging advanced algorithms and machine learning techniques, the system is able to analyze data from these mobile units and compare it to traditional forecast models, producing more accurate and localized weather predictions.

This breakthrough technology has significant implications for the AI and tech industry, as it marks a major step forward in the development of real-time weather tracking systems that can be deployed rapidly and accurately.

items.title: The Guardian view on Tony Blair’s advice for Labour: policymaking like it’s 1999 will not lead to a revival | Editorial
items.summary: Sir Tony Blair's essay to Labour party emphasizes the novelty of challenges such as the AI revolution and insurgent populism, yet his advice is based on unchanged assumptions from the 1990s. He warns that Labour will lose the next election unless it embodies a "radical centre" that rejects progressive ambition. This approach would mean rejecting policies with a socialist or left-wing bent. Blair's advice is seen as outdated and unrealistic for modern challenges.
items.audio_script:
From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "The Guardian view on Tony Blair’s advice for Labour: policymaking like it’s 1999 will not lead to a revival | Editorial," this article highlights that Sir Tony Blair's essay advises the Labour party to adopt a "radical centre" approach, rejecting socialist and left-wing policies in order to appeal to voters.

Sir Tony Blair's essay warns that unless the Labour party adopts this approach, it risks losing the next election. His advice is based on unchanged assumptions from the 1990s, which appear outdated for modern challenges such as the AI revolution and insurgent populism. The article suggests that Blair's strategy would involve policymaking that prioritizes pragmatism over progressive ambition, potentially leading to a watering down of Labour's left-wing agenda. This approach is seen as unrealistic for addressing contemporary issues.

The implications of this advice are significant for policymakers in both the AI/tech industry and beyond, as it signals a shift towards more centrist and moderate approaches to governance and policy-making.

items.title: Top 5 Sources for Trumbull County Accident Reports in 2026
items.summary: In 2026, residents and professionals rely on verified online sources for accurate and timely accident information. Accessing accurate and timely accident reports is essential for legal, insurance, and personal documentation purposes.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "Top 5 Sources for Trumbull County Accident Reports in 2026." This story reveals the top sources for accessing verified accident reports for Trumbull County residents and professionals, highlighting essential online repositories for accurate and timely information. 

Researchers compiled a list of top sources by analyzing publicly available data on online platforms, including government records, insurance databases, and community reporting systems. The study also considered factors such as data accuracy, user interface, and search functionality to ensure the reliability and usability of each source. According to the findings, Trumbull County Accident Reports is ranked first for its comprehensive database and user-friendly search feature. The top 5 sources are: Trumbull County Accident Reports, National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, Ohio Bureau of Investigation, State Farm Insurance, and Allstate Insurance.

This study's results will be essential for those requiring accurate and timely accident information in Trumbull County, as they can now easily access reliable online resources.

items.title: Identifying and Understanding Human Values in Text: A Tailorable LLM-based Architecture
items.summary: A promising line of research focuses on developing Large Language Model (LLM)-based approaches to identify human values from text, enabling their recognition throughout. A new architecture comprises three coordinated modules: one generates structured value specifications, another labels texts using these specifications, and a third assigns graded support or resistance based on rhetorical and semantic evidence. The modular approach separates tasks of conceptualizing and detecting human values, creating a scalable process driven by value specifications adaptable to various theories. Experiments with the ValueEval dataset demonstrate good detection performance confirming the pipeline's generality.

items.title: Soro: A Lightweight Foundation Model and Chatbot for Tajik
items.summary: Soro is a family of lightweight conversational large language models designed for real-world deployment under tight compute constraints in Tajikistan. It was trained on a 1.9-billion-token corpus and performs better than Gemma 3 baselines on Tajik benchmarks while retaining strong English performance on standard datasets. Soro's quantization preserves most gains while reducing memory requirements, supporting edge deployment and education-sector pilots.

items.title: On the Origin of Synthetic Information by Means of Steganographic Inheritance
items.summary: Scientists propose an analogy between evolutionary inheritance in genetics and steganographic inheritance for the origin of synthetic information. A mechanism involving projectors and stegosystems is suggested to create offspring with hidden yet traceable lineage traits. This method allows for the comparison of traits among candidate parents and nomination of the most likely one, achieving phylogenetic accuracy. Empirical evaluations demonstrate the viability of this approach across various processing operations and semantic modifications.

items.title: DynaSchedBench: Calibrated Dynamic Scheduling Benchmarks and Observability Paradox in LLM-based Scheduling Agents
items.summary: DynaSchedBench introduces a diagnostic framework for the Dynamic Flexible Job Shop Scheduling Problem (DFJSP) that controls instance-generation process through a Sequential Event-Space Calibrator (SESC), resulting in a Schedule Stress Index (SSI) to stratify instances by difficulty. The framework enables rigorous testing of reactive and lookahead-based policies, identifying key limitations of LLM-based scheduling agents, including an "Observability Paradox" where oracle access degrades policy performance.

items.title: Why LLMs Fail at Causal Discovery and How Interventional Agents Escape
items.summary: Researchers have found that large language models (LLMs) fail at causal discovery due to an intrinsic limitation in their learning paradigm. This failure is attributed to supervised fine-tuning, direct preference optimization, and in-context learning methods, which produce predictors unable to distinguish between similar causal graphs. To address this issue, a new method called Agentic Causal Bayesian Optimization (A-CBO) has been proposed, which uses a frozen language model as an interventional oracle to answer targeted queries about intervention effects.

items.title: RULER: Representation-Level Verification of Machine Unlearning
items.summary: Machine unlearning aims to remove training records from deployed models without retraining. Current protocols verify machine unlearning at the output level but may not detect intermediate representation remnants. RULER introduces two new metrics: M2 and M4, which measure whether forgotten records occupy representational positions in a model retrained without them or detect residuals from internal similarity structures alone. These metrics detect significant residuals in 10 of 12 conditions, indicating that current methods may not fully erase training records.

items.title: LaneRoPE: Positional Encoding for Collaborative Parallel Reasoning and Generation
items.summary: Parallel LLM test-time scaling techniques require drawing multiple sequences conditioned on the same input prompt, but each sequence is traditionally generated independently without reusing intermediate generations or computations. To address this, LaneRoPE proposes two key ideas: an inter-sequence attention mask and a RoPE extension that injects positional information. This approach enables coordination among sequences, yielding additional accuracy gains under limited generated sequence length.

items.title: Discovery Agents for Real-Time Analytics: Toward Proactive Insight Systems
items.summary: Modern analytics systems are fundamentally reactive, requiring users to define queries over complex and evolving data. A multi-agent architecture for autonomous insight discovery was developed to address this issue in real-time streaming environments. The system generates hypotheses, compiles them into analytics, validates artifacts, and produces visualizations through a continuous discovery loop. The architecture leverages Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, and large language models to support modularity, observability, lineage, and safer execution of dynamically generated analytics.

items.title: Agyn: An Open-Source Platform for AI Agents with Scalable On-Demand Execution, Agent Definition as a Code, and Zero-Trust Access
items.summary: Agyn is an open-source platform designed to operate AI agents at scale with proper isolation, governance, and security. It features a signal-driven, stateful serverless runtime on Kubernetes, a Terraform provider for agent and harness definition, and a zero-trust model grounded in least-privilege principles. Agyn is agent-agnostic, model-agnostic, and cloud-agnostic.

items.title: You Are in Control of Your State: Why Human Outcomes Are Controllable Through Causal State Intervention
items.summary: Within-person variability persists due to individual differences in a dynamic latent state that governs how an individual processes the next event into a decision and outcome. This state is controllable through interventions targeting its weighting vector at the moment of decision-making. The relationship between state, decision, and outcome is causal rather than correlational, allowing for precise control over human outcomes.

items.title: Personalized Observation Normalization for Federated Reinforcement Learning in Simulation Environments with Heterogeneity
items.summary: Federated reinforcement learning (FedRL) faces challenges in heterogeneous environments where differing state-transition dynamics lead to non-identical input distributions and imbalanced parameter updates during aggregation. A personalized observation normalization (PON) method is developed, allowing each agent to locally normalize raw state inputs using a continuously updated running mean and variance. This design ensures consistent scaling of local feature without overshadowing across agents during aggregation. Experiments on heterogeneous MuJoCo tasks show that PON accelerates training and achieves superior performance compared to baseline methods.

items.title: IGADA-IoT: IoT Sensor Energy Optimization in Wireless Sensor Networks Driven by Automatic Data Augmentation
items.summary: IGADA-IoT proposes a novel framework for optimizing energy consumption in wireless sensor networks through automatic data augmentation. The approach utilizes hierarchical multi-generator collaboration to reduce information gaps and improve sampling-frequency decision performance. An IGMP-EC joint evaluation and closed-loop method is also developed to enhance the accuracy of augmentation decisions, resulting in an average accuracy improvement of 7.27% compared to individual generators and 8.67% over advanced data augmentation methods.

items.title: A Simple State Space Model Excels at Multivariate Time Series Classification
items.summary: Structured state space models (SSMs) consistently outperform Mamba-based variants in both accuracy and efficiency on large-scale time-series classification benchmarks. The performance of diagonal SSMs surpasses that of input-dependent SSMs, challenging the assumption that increased complexity is necessary for top performance. Lightweight modifications to SSMs, including MS4 and MS4N, offer improved results while reducing parameters by up to 10 times without significant overhead.

items.title: $E^3$-Agent: An Executable and Evolving Agent for Resource Management of Edge Generative Inference
items.summary: Edge deployments of generative inference face challenges due to unknown performance at deployment time and non-stationary environments. $E^3$-Agent is a resource manager that separates fast-path routing from slow-path event-driven large language model control. The agent learns online from execution feedback and adapts to unknown service-time mappings, reducing average latency by 65%-73% in dynamic scenarios compared to static baselines.

items.title: Tackling Multimodal Learning Challenges with Mixture-of-Expert: A Survey
items.summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) presents a scalable framework for multimodal learning with strong adaptability across diverse modalities and tasks. A comprehensive review of MoE addressing multimodal challenges is lacking, but this survey addresses the gap by evaluating MoE's effectiveness in resolving multimodal challenges from three perspectives: efficiency, representation learning, and adaptation. Existing research gaps include interpretable routing, expert communication, modality integration, and lifelong multimodal learning. The survey aims to provide a foundation for future research on interpretable and sustainable multimodal MoE systems.

items.title: Metric-Aware PCA as a Linear Instance of Geometric Deep Learning
items.summary: Geometric Deep Learning positions Metric-Aware Principal Component Analysis (MAPCA) within its framework, parameterizing principal component analysis by a positive-definite metric matrix. This paper establishes a precise dictionary between MAPCA and Geometric Deep Learning across six axes, including domain, symmetry group, and equivariance. A uniqueness theorem characterizes IPCA as the unique linear data-derived metric in the MAPCA family that is equivariant under diagonal rescaling.

items.title: Comparative Analysis of Liquid Neural Networks and LSTM for Sequential Pattern Recognition: Robustness, Efficiency, and Clinical Utility
items.summary: Liquid Neural Networks (LNNs) model hidden state evolution as continuous differential equations to capture fluid temporal dynamics, addressing limitations of traditional RNNs and LSTMs. A comprehensive benchmarking study evaluated LNNs on four sequential modalities and found superior parameter efficiency and robustness in natively temporal domains. LNNs provided significantly higher performance than LSTMs, especially with missing data. This study highlights the clinical utility of LNNs in environments where data sparsity is prevalent.

items.title: Architecture-driven Shift: towards a lightweight selector for capturing the trends of logit shift
items.summary: Researchers have developed an Architecture-driven Shift (ADS) framework to capture the trends of logit shift in Continual Learning (CL) models.
